title: ailanthus integrifolia subsp. calycina
intro: Ailanthus integrifolia subsp. calycina, also known as the Chinese tree of heaven, is a subspecies of the ailanthus tree, native to China and widely cultivated in various parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, bark, and roots, which contain saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ailanthus integrifolia subsp. calycina are said to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
